order: A protected release update runs the complete second-candidate lifecycle regression locally before the remote release gate; the Runner cannot reuse an older enforced record or treat PR/main CI as a substitute for that preflight.
Runner phases consume resolved procedural guidance but cannot weaken AGENTS.MD invariants or reinterpret an invalid local instruction as absent.

These seven compatibility phases remain the fallback unless the exact repository has passed externally trusted, candidate-bound headless Activation; the paused Control Center is not an execution prerequisite and cannot grant release authority.

A protected runtime change must prove exact same-range parent document synchronization, environment-aware isolation refusal or real containment, the focused Next Workflow aggregate, and ordinary PR/main CI without treating skipped unavailable-environment execution as production evidence; live provider discovery additionally proves its default read-only-root/private-tmpfs executable binding on a prerequisite-complete host.

Bootstrap, installed-launcher integrity, injected provider-discovery, and development-selection regressions may use complete private non-executed provider descriptors on CI without Codex; release proof still requires the actual installed, pinned, certified, and observed native provider, and missing real containment explicitly skips positive bootstrap and launcher integrity while guided refusal passes.
